title: Call for Applications: Hub Peer-Learning Visit to Lisbon
content:
The EU-funded European Heritage Hub invites heritage professionals and representatives of cities and regions to apply for its upcoming Peer-Learning Visit in Lisbon, Portugal from 4-6 November 2026. Organised in partnership with the City of Lisbon, this programme offers the opportunity for meaningful exchanges among cultural heritage sector peers in addition to participants aiming to learn more about ways to transform their own heritage initiatives and policies.

The visit builds on the Hub’s Open Call for Local Good Practices, launched in November 2025, which recognised exemplary local solutions in cultural heritage that contribute to the green, digital, and social transformation of our society. Lisbon’s “Memories of Lisbon” was among the 10 selected practices. This project addresses the risk of social isolation among older residents by engaging high school students to record and digitise their life stories, creating a powerful intergenerational bridge and a shared digital archive.
snippet: The EU-funded European Heritage Hub invites heritage professionals and representatives of cities and regions to apply for its upcoming Peer-Learning Visit in Lisbon, Portugal from 4-6 November 2026.
creator: Liv Martin

title: Call for Applications: Small Grants Scheme for heritage-related projects led by civil society in EU candidate and neighbouring countries
content:
Opening date: 20 July – Closing date: 20 September 2026

The EU-funded European Heritage Hub aims to empower heritage stakeholders from candidate and neighbouring countries of the European Union to become active protagonists of the wider European heritage movement, thereby enhancing the ever-closer relationship between these countries and the European Union. As part of this mission, the Hub is launching the second edition of its Small Grants Scheme, with support from the ALIPH Foundation.

Civil society from 11 neighbouring countries of the European Union (Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo*, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia and Ukraine) are eligible to apply.

  • Building the capacity of civil society;
  • Enhancing the key role of cultural heritage for the triple transformation (green, digital and social);
  • Strengthening democracy, the rule of law and other fundamental rights and values;
  • Building peace and stability, through enhancing intercultural and interreligious dialogue and reconciliation.
snippet: Opening date: 20 July – Closing date: 20 September 2026
creator: Liv Martin
